references.title: Extracting Course Similarity Signal using Subword Embeddings

extracted.Initialization Method: The aprroach relies on creating course embeddings - Random initialization
extracted.Preprocessing Details: More than just using Course Descriptions (Course2Vec), the authors also include information about the teaching staff.
extracted.Comments:
Authors explore 3 RQs meant to see whether: 

  1. FastText is better than other representations, 

2. CompareFastText vs skip-grams

3. Adding course demographics improve or benefir course equivalency

Suthors use sequence of enrollments to create the embedings.
extracted.Limitations:
Does not consider the use of ensemble methods. 

No analysis of temporal drift

Possible co-variates that could bias results.

Does not consider prior knowledge

annotations.text: Authors use different NLP on course names as part of the features in a course sequence recommender system. Results show that naming convention actually is useful for the models and the authors expand in the different ways that similarity can be computed to measure distance between courses.
